description: Identity passthrough with tilize/untilize - reader reads input sticks/tiles, compute tilizes then untilizes (RM) or passes through (TILE), writer writes output

approach: RM path: read 32 sticks per tile-row via TensorAccessor, push Wt pages to cb_in_rm. TILE path: read tiles one at a time to cb_in via TensorAccessor.
RM: tilize cb_in_rm->cb_out then untilize cb_out->cb_out_rm using helpers. TILE: copy_tile from cb_in to cb_out tile-by-tile with manual DST management.
RM: wait Wt pages from cb_out_rm, write 32 sticks per tile-row via TensorAccessor. TILE: wait 1 tile from cb_out, write via TensorAccessor tile-by-tile.

cb_summary: RM: c0:Wt/Wt c16:Wt/Wt(internal) c17:Wt/Wt. TILE: c1:1/1 c16:1/1

details: TILE path: copy_tile_to_dst_init_short and copy_tile not declared - missing include for tile_move_copy API

description: copy_tile and copy_tile_to_dst_init_short require include of api/compute/tile_move_copy.h and api/compute/pack.h
evidence: compiler error: copy_tile_to_dst_init_short was not declared in this scope

change: Added missing includes: api/compute/tile_move_copy.h, api/compute/pack.h, api/compute/cb_api.h for TILE path copy_tile and pack_tile functions

details: 7/8 passed, rm-multi_batch (4,2,64,64) hangs. TIMEOUT on device. Physical cores (x=19,y=26) and (x=18,y=26) stuck.
suspected_cause: RM reader iterates num_rows tile-rows but each tile-row reads 32 sticks. For shape (4,2,64,64), num_rows=NC*Ht=8*2=16, total sticks=16*32=512, but actual sticks=4*2*64=512 - that matches. The issue might be that for multi-Ht shapes, the tilize helper processes num_rows blocks where each block is 1 tile-row high, but untilize also processes num_rows blocks. Need to check if untilize waits for Wt tiles per block but tilize only pushed Wt per block.

description: RM path: tilize runs for ALL num_rows blocks before untilize starts, but cb_out only holds Wt pages. After first block, cb_out is full and tilize blocks. Need to interleave tilize and untilize per tile-row.
evidence: Hang only on multi_batch shape (4,2,64,64) which has num_rows=16 but cb_out capacity=Wt=2 pages. Single-tile/non-square shapes have num_rows=1 so no overflow.

change: Interleave tilize and untilize per tile-row in a loop instead of sequential bulk calls. Use InitOnly/Neither/UninitOnly pattern for init lifecycle across iterations.

approach: Simplified RM path to use InitAndUninit per iteration - tilize and untilize one tile-row at a time in a loop

design_deviations: Used InitAndUninit per tile-row iteration instead of bulk tilize/untilize to avoid cb_out overflow

description: Add square + reduce_row phases in compute. Output is mean(x^2) per row with tile-aligned reduced shape. Reader fills cb_scaler with 1/W. Writer outputs 1 tile per row.

change: Changed output_shape to list(shape[:-1]) + [32] for stage 2 reduced output
reason: Stage 2 outputs mean(x^2) which has reduced width dimension (1 tile = 32 elements)

change: Compute Wt_out from output tensor shape for writer runtime args instead of using input Wt
reason: Stage 2 has reduced output width (32 vs input W), writer needs output Wt=1 not input Wt

change: Fixed output_stick_size to use output_W instead of input W
reason: Stage 2 output has reduced width (32), stick size must match output not input

change: Updated cb16 pages to Wt_out, cb17 total_size to Wt_out*tile_size, computed Wt_out early, removed duplicate output_W definition
reason: All output CBs and writer args must use output width tiles not input width tiles for reduced output stage

approach: Added prepare_reduce_scaler<cb_scaler>(1/W) at startup to fill scaler CB for reduce phase. Input reading unchanged from Stage 1.
Per tile-row: tilize(RM)->square(cb_in->cb_x_sq)->reduce<SUM,REDUCE_ROW>(cb_x_sq->cb_out)->untilize(RM). Output 1 tile per row to cb_out. hw_startup with cb_in,cb_scaler,cb_out.

cb_summary: c0:Wt/Wt c1:Wt/Wt(perTile) c2:Wt/Wt(perTile) c8:1/persist c16:1/1 c17:1/1

details: TypeError: __getitem__() incompatible args - ttnn Shape does not support slice syntax shape[:-1]

description: ttnn.Shape does not support slice syntax [:-1]. Need to convert to list first.

notes: Two-pass reader, 8 compute phases, CB layout validated

description: Square input tiles and reduce_row to compute mean(x^2). Reduced output shape.

approach: Already has square(cb_in->cb_x_sq) + reduce_row(cb_x_sq->cb_out) from builder. Untilize uses Wt_out=1.

cb_summary: c0:Wt/Wt c1:Wt/Wt c2:Wt/Wt c8:1/persist c16:1/1 c17:1/1

details: tile-single_tile PASSED, tile-multi_tile (1,1,64,128) HANG/timeout. 1 passed, 1 failed before abort.

description: cb_x_sq (c2) has only 2 pages but square pushes Wt tiles sequentially before reduce consumes them. For Wt>2, cb_reserve_back blocks because CB is full and no consumer is running (both on compute RISC). Fix: increase cb_x_sq to Wt pages in program descriptor.
evidence: TRISC0 stuck in reduce cb_wait_front on cb_x_sq -- square blocked on cb_reserve_back, never completed pushing all Wt tiles. Only works for Wt=1 (single_tile passes, multi_tile hangs).

change: Increased cb_x_sq (c2) from 2 pages to Wt pages
reason: square and reduce both run on compute RISC sequentially - square must push all Wt tiles before reduce starts consuming. With only 2 pages, cb_reserve_back blocks for Wt>2.

change: cb_x_sq total_size changed from 2*tile_size to Wt*tile_size

upstream_fixes: cb_x_sq increased from 2 to Wt pages
